pull_requests.title: Reduce fetching pos data to 24h
pull_requests.body: This PR updates the dj_fetching_data example to reduce fetching position data to a 24h window instead of the first 3 days. This 24h window is aligned with the start of either `light_on` or `light_off` depending on whichever happened first.

pull_requests.title: Support optional inclusion of `start`, `end` boundaries in  `load`
pull_requests.body:
Datetime range querying is fundamental to aeon datasets, so here we introduce optional parameters for precisely specifying how to deal with range boundaries, similar to the [pandas.date_range](https://pandas.pydata.org/pandas-docs/stable/reference/api/pandas.date_range.html) and [pandas.DataFrame.between_time](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.DataFrame.between_time.html) functions, which also have an inclusive parameter.

Unit tests have been added to cover possible edge cas

- [x] Other

**Why is this PR needed?**
In case the movement website is not available and needs to be redeployed, `linkcheck` will fail on these external URL references (see for example the recent [linkcheck loop](https://github.com/neuroinformatics-unit/movement/actions/runs/16449971843/job/46492310327) which prevented the docs from being redeployed). The better option here is hence to use "internal" lin

pull_requests.title: Require users to specify artifact.ci mode in `build_sphinx_docs` action
pull_requests.body:
## Description

**What is this PR**

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Addition of a new feature
- [ ] Other

**Why is this PR needed?**

**What does this PR do?**
This PR changes the `use-artifact` input in [build_sphinx_docs](https://github.com/neuroinformatics-unit/actions/tree/main/build_sphinx_docs) to require users to specify the `artifactci-mode` to use with artifact-ci.
The available options are:
- eager: upload the files within the artifact immediately
- lazy: do not upload the artifact

pull_requests.title: Refactor clean_swaps
pull_requests.body:
## Fill early frames
In #484 the lines to fill early frames (before the first complete frame) were removed from `clean_swaps`. This means that if the input df starts with only one animal being detected for the first 1000 frames, these frames will be dropped (NaN) in the cleaned df. This PR reintroduces "filling early frames". 

## Merging
When merging the cleaned df with the original df any single-detection swaps will be dropped because we merge based on "identity_name":
https://github.com/

Replaces remaining numpy legacy random generation with Generator ([`numpy.random.default_rng()`](https://numpy.org/doc/2.2/reference/random/generator.html#numpy.random.default_rng))

## References
#596 

## How has this PR been tested?
Added rng fixture and updated tests 

## Is this a breaking change?
No, but the

pull_requests.title: Temporarily suppress napari warnings
pull_requests.body:
100s of warnings (UserWarning #573 and FutureWarning #593)  were introduced in PR #476 and these obfuscate pytest's warnings summary, making it difficult to see if new changes are potentially introducing issues.

This PR filters out the aforementioned warnings.

pull_requests.title: Reorganise sections
pull_requests.body:
This PR addresses #93 #94 #96.
- "To the Introduction" is now "Get started" on the home page ([preview](https://lochhh.github.io/aeon_docs/))
- The heading "Tutorial" for the multi-animal tracking and neuropixels recording pages is now "Key features". These two pages are also moved out of the User Guide and are now subpages of Getting started ([preview](https://lochhh.github.io/aeon_docs/getting_started/index.html))
- The section for Repositories is now also a subpage of Getting started, alon

pull_requests.title: Add xrefs to docfx.json
pull_requests.body:
This PR adds a few xrefs for DocFX, but the `xref` argument is not currently used since the .md files are generated using `docfx metadata` instead of `docfx build` where the `xref` argument is applied (see [previous conversation](https://github.com/SainsburyWellcomeCentre/aeon_docs/pull/103#pullrequestreview-2593329493)). 
 I can't find any xrefmap files for [mathdotnet](https://numerics.mathdotnet.com/api/) and [mysqlconnector](https://mysqlconnector.net/api) though.

**What does this PR do?**
### Custom loguru-based `MovementLogger`
This PR replaces the use of the `logging` module with [`loguru`](https://loguru.readthedocs.io/en/stable/). It extends loguru's `logger` with a custom `MovementLogger` that overrides `logger.error` and `logger.exception` to return the Exception b

pull_requests.title: Remove link check for dotnet API
pull_requests.body:
As we have too many references to `"https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/.*"`, docs build often [fails](https://github.com/lochhh/aeon_docs/actions/runs/13119182170/job/36600807902) due to 429 Client Error: Too Many Requests for url. 

In this PR we configure linkcheck to ignore the dotnet API website.

pull_requests.title: Questions and comments
pull_requests.body:
This PR attempts to structure each acquisition module by Bonsai Nodes. The updated template structure is as follows: 
```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[Device/Module] --> B1[Nodes]
    A --> B2[GUI]
    A --> B3[Logging]
    A --> B4[State persistence]
    A --> B5[Alerts]
    B1 --> C1[Node1]
    B1 --> C2[Node2]
    C1 --> D1[Inputs]
    C1 --> D2[Outputs]
    C1 --> D3[Properties]
    C1 --> D4[Usage]
    D3 --> E1[General]
    D3 --> E2[Others]
    D3 --> E3[Subjects]
    E3 -->
